content: Recap of our October general meeting at the Franklin Park Conservatory. Dorothy Kendall gave a hands-on talk on dividing mature Cattleya, and members brought blooming plants for the show table. Notes and the next meeting date are below.
excerpt: October meeting recap: dividing Cattleya and a packed show table.
title: October Meeting Recap: Dividing Cattleya

content: A step-by-step guide to repotting Phalaenopsis after bloom. We cover when to repot, choosing fir-bark vs. sphagnum, trimming dead roots, and settling the plant back into a clean pot. Photos from the workshop are in the gallery.
excerpt: When and how to repot your Phalaenopsis once the flowers fade.
title: Repotting Phalaenopsis After Bloom

content: Paphiopedilum can be tricky on a windowsill, so many members grow them under lights. This article covers fixture choice, photoperiod, distance from the canopy, and reading leaf color to dial in intensity.
excerpt: Tips for growing Paphiopedilum under LED and fluorescent lights.
title: Growing Paphiopedilum Under Lights

content: Results from the 2026 Columbus Orchid Show this past March. Best in Show went to a stunning Cattleya from a longtime member, and our youth division saw record entries. Full ribbon list and judges' notes inside.
excerpt: Best in Show, ribbon winners, and highlights from the 2026 show.
title: 2026 Columbus Orchid Show Results

content: Membership renewals for the new society year are now open. Dues remain unchanged, and renewing members keep access to the lending library and quarterly plant raffles. Renew online or bring a check to the next meeting.
excerpt: Renew your society membership for the new year.
title: Membership Renewals Now Open

content: Draft notes for an upcoming article on coaxing reluctant Phalaenopsis into spike. Covering the autumn temperature drop, watering changes, and patience. Not ready to publish yet.
excerpt: Work in progress: encouraging Phalaenopsis to spike.
title: Encouraging Phalaenopsis to Spike
